paragraphs: I'm drawn to the kind of problem where the data exists but the answer isn't obvious — where you have to ask the right question before you can write a single line of code. Whether it's untangling messy, multi-source datasets or stress-testing a model's assumptions, I find the most satisfaction in the detective work that happens before any chart gets built.

My approach leans on rigor first and storytelling second: the analysis only earns the right to tell a story once the numbers hold up. That discipline, built across financial services, healthcare, and retail domains, is what I bring to every project — the habit of asking 'what would break this conclusion?' before presenting it.
